source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from Cal Poly CDS Rev.14 compliant 1U CubeSat practice: four +Z-protruding corner rails (hard-anodized), four side panels with PC/104 cutouts, +Z and -Z face plates, internal PC/104 payload-tray midplate, deployment-switch bracket on -Z face, remove-before-flight pin mount, and M3 stainless fastener set. Load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds inherit verbatim from the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design the primary structure of a 1U CubeSat compliant with the Cal Poly CubeSat Design Specification (CDS) Rev. 14. The CubeSat must fit inside a 100.0 mm x 100.0 mm x 113.5 mm envelope (accounting for standoff rails and access port allowances per CDS). Total mass must not exceed 1.33 kg (1U CDS limit). The four +Z rails must protrude 6.5 mm above the +Z face and act as the deployer rail contact surface; the rails must be anodized hard-anodize type III to prevent cold welding with the P-POD/ISIPOD deployer. Material is typically Al 6061-T6 or Al 7075-T7351 for the primary structure. Launch environment for a typical ride-share is random vibration 14.1 Grms (NASA GEVS, 20-2000 Hz) and a quasi-static limit load of 25 g in each axis. The first natural frequency of the assembled CubeSat must be above 100 Hz to decouple from the deployer during launch vibration. Stress allowables per NASA-STD-5001B: yield FoS 2.0 (higher for CubeSats because the structure doubles as the primary load path and has minimal test coverage), ultimate FoS 2.6. Primary verification is structural FEA (linear static at 25 g triaxial) plus modal analysis for first frequency and random-vibration harmonic response for 3-sigma stress evaluation. Vibration is a primary gate. Thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are explicitly NOT required at this structural gate. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the 1U structure, a mass budget, and a report listing total mass in g, first modal frequency in Hz, max stress at 25 g in MPa with FoS, and 3-sigma random vib stress in MPa.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
This 1U CubeSat is delivered as a CDS-compliant multi-part bolted assembly. The speculative decomposition is: (A) four +Z-protruding corner rails hard-anodized type III, (B) four side panels (+/-X and +/-Y) with PC/104 cutouts and camera access ports, (C) +Z and -Z deck plates (top cap and bottom cap), (D) internal PC/104 payload-tray midplate mounting the stacked PC/104 board stack, (E) deployment-switch bracket on -Z face, (F) remove-before-flight (RBF) pin mount, and (G) M3 stainless fastener set with Helicoil inserts. Inter-family interfaces: rails-to-side-panels bolted along rail flange, side-panels-to-deck-plates bolted perimeter, rails-to-deck-plates bolted at rail ends, midplate-to-rails via threaded standoffs, deployment-switch-bracket-to-side-panel bolted. FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED model only — per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part DCR across bolted joints must be ≤ 1.0 under all three 25 g axis load cases and LC4 random vib 3-sigma. Rail protrusion and rail anodize spec are verified at assembled level by geometric inspection. All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original single-part brief are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criteria are added.
short_prompt:
Design the primary structure of a 1U CubeSat per Cal Poly CubeSat Design Specification (CDS) Rev. 14, delivered as a CDS-compliant bolted multi-part assembly: four +Z-protruding corner rails hard-anodized type III + four PC/104 side panels + +Z and -Z deck plates + internal PC/104 payload-tray midplate + deployment-switch bracket + RBF pin mount + M3 stainless fastener set with Helicoil inserts. Envelope 100.0 x 100.0 x 113.5 mm; total mass <= 1.33 kg. Rails protrude 6.5 mm above the +Z face to contact the P-POD/ISIPOD deployer and must be hard-anodized type III to prevent cold welding. Material typically Al 6061-T6 or Al 7075-T7351. Stress allowables per NASA-STD-5001B: yield FoS 2.0, ultimate FoS 2.6.

Load cases.
- LC1-LC3 quasi-static: 25 g in each axis.
- LC4 random vibration: 14.1 Grms NASA GEVS 20-2000 Hz.
- LC5 modal: first natural frequency > 100 Hz (deployer decouple).

Inter-part DCR across bolted joints <= 1.0 under all three 25 g axes and LC4 random-vib 3-sigma. Rail protrusion and anodize spec verified at assembled level by geometric inspection.

prompt.title: CDS-compliant 1U CubeSat structure (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Design a 1U CubeSat as a bolted assembly of four hard-anodized corner rails, four side panels, +/-Z deck plates, internal PC/104 midplate, deployment-switch bracket, RBF-pin mount, and M3 fasteners. Fits 100 x 100 x 113.5 mm envelope, ≤ 1.33 kg; survives 14.1 Grms + 25 g triaxial; first mode ≥ 100 Hz. FEM at assembled level only; connection DCR ≤ 1.0.

geometric_constraints.pc104_stack_pattern: PC/104 with M3 through-holes on 4-corner pattern

parts.name: +Z-protruding corner rail
parts.role: deployer-contact rail at each vertical edge; protrudes 6.5 mm above +Z face; primary launch-load path into structure
parts.material_hint: Al 7075-T7351 machined, hard-anodized type III on rail contact face to prevent cold welding with deployer

parts.name: side panel
parts.role: +/-X and +/-Y face panel; provides mounting for solar cells and camera access ports; shears load between rails
parts.material_hint: Al 6061-T6 sheet, hard-anodized type III (non-rail surfaces)

parts.name: deck plate (+Z top cap / -Z bottom cap)
parts.role: top and bottom end-caps; bottom cap carries -Z deployment switch and remove-before-flight pin; top cap carries access ports

parts.name: internal PC/104 midplate / payload tray
parts.role: supports PC/104 payload-card stack; decouples payload mass from rails via threaded standoffs
parts.material_hint: Al 6061-T6 plate with Helicoil M3 inserts

parts.name: deployment-switch bracket
parts.role: holds micro switch on -Z face; depressed by P-POD spring, triggers spacecraft power-on at ejection
parts.material_hint: Al 6061-T6 sheet, bent; switch per CDS-compliant vendor list

parts.name: remove-before-flight (RBF) pin mount
parts.role: through-panel pin socket accessible while CubeSat is stowed in deployer; cuts battery circuit during integration
parts.material_hint: Al 6061-T6 sheet with nylon RBF pin

parts.name: M3 fastener set with Helicoil inserts
parts.role: secures all structural joints; stainless steel with locking feature per NASA-STD-5020B
parts.material_hint: A2 or A4 stainless, self-locking or thread-locked with Loctite 243

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: CDS convention: origin at -X-Y-Z corner of reference envelope, Z along rails toward deployer door

pass_fail_criteria.description: DCR across rail-side-panel, side-panel-deck-plate, rail-deck-plate, and midplate-standoff bolted interfaces ≤ 1.0 under worst-case LC1/LC2/LC3 combined with LC4 3-sigma.
Assembled rail-protrusion height and anodize coverage verified by geometric inspection; no structural interference between payload stack and inner surface of side panels under LC4.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

interfaces.load_path: Rail flange bolts to side-panel edge along rail length; primary shear path from rail to side panel
Rail ends bolt to +Z and -Z deck plates at corner bosses; carries axial Z load
Side-panel edges bolted to deck plates along perimeter; seals bay and carries shear
Internal midplate mounted to rails via threaded M3 standoffs through rail inner face
Deployment-switch bracket bolted to -Z side panel (or -Z deck)
RBF pin mount bolted to side panel; pin transfers only its own small inertia

fem_check_policy.rationale: 1U CubeSat first-mode frequency is strongly sensitive to rail-side-panel bolt stiffness and midplate standoff layout. Per-part stress cannot capture the deployer-decoupling mode or the PC/104 stack sway mode that CDS Rev.14 compliance requires.

notes.why_multi_part: CDS Rev.14 compliance essentially mandates a multi-part bolted structure: rails must be separately anodized, the PC/104 payload tray must be removable for integration, and deployment-switch and RBF-pin mounts occupy dedicated brackets. No monolithic 1U structure is CDS-compliant in practice.
notes.speculation_caveats: Part counts and dimensional ranges reflect common Type-III-anodized Al 7075 1U designs (e.g. ISIS 1U, Pumpkin CubeSat Kit, UNISEC Type-B). Exact rail cross-section and midplate cutout pattern depend on the specific PC/104 bus selected.

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
